mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE is below 5.0, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising industrial production and expanding real retail sales indicates synchronized supply-demand recovery, which validates corporate profit growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO change × RSAFS change) shows no statistically significant predictive power for 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10), or the joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, an increase in housing starts signals resilient consumer demand that outpaces price pressures, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity markets price in soft-landing scenarios.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of high-inflation observations, or the conditional IC is indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and operational efficiency, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-month correlation between INDPRO month-over-month changes and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10 for two consecutive years, indicating structural decoupling.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, the cost of capital compresses equity valuations and slows economic activity, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings growth decelerates.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the predictive Rank IC is consistently below 0.02 across multiple tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in nonfarm payrolls and real retail sales confirms broad-based economic acceleration, which triggers institutional buying and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The cross-product of monthly PAYEMS and RSAFS changes shows no significant forward predictive power for 20-day returns (p > 0.05), or the signal generates a negative information ratio over a 4-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary environment, a rebound in industrial production signals the trough of the business cycle and anticipates policy support, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets front-run the recovery.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 contracts for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds fail to precede positive 5-day forward returns in more than 65% of identified recessionary troughs, or the conditional forward return distribution is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).
